mnemonics: People Hate Buying Dull Cables (Physical Layer Functions: Physical topology, Hardware, Bandwidth, Data rate, Channel capacity)
keyPoints: The Physical Layer is responsible for the transmission and reception of raw bit streams over a physical medium.
It defines the electrical, mechanical, procedural, and functional specifications for activating, maintaining, and deactivating the physical link.
Key concepts include data rate (bandwidth), bit synchronization, physical topologies (mesh, star, bus, ring), and transmission media (twisted-pair, coaxial, fiber optic).
commonMistakes: Confusing the Physical Layer (Layer 1) with the Data Link Layer (Layer 2), which handles framing and error control.
Assuming all transmission media have the same properties, such as latency, bandwidth, and susceptibility to interference.
Thinking the Physical Layer is only about cables; it also includes specifications for wireless radio frequencies, infrared signals, and hardware interfaces like NICs and hubs.
